pages.text:
April 3rd 1992.
    Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, Peter
John & Charles set off after lunch to the
camping shop at Wolverhampton. We bought
a new Igloo Tent, Korean-designed
knuckle joints - which only takes "2 minutes
to erect.
    An array of problems – the
bracket below a water tank has
broken – there has been 2 lots of
clanking – the Quinto campsite wouldn't
have us because we've not members.
The next campsite was too small.
We have ended up at __Ellesmere__ __Tetchill__, where
Eve has broken the corkscrew &
the van heater doesn't work. Ice
formed in the night & most of us
were cold.
    Sunshine at the moment, inspite
of gloomy weather for days. The campsite
is expensive (£10) a key for the loos

+ laundry – v. clean. Well laid out in
a little grassy field right in the
middle of town. Ellesmere College is
right on the doorstep.

→ April 4th.
    This campsite is so good that
I am upping it to *****. It is
clean + right near town. Walking to
shops & restaurants is easy. It's good
for the children to play football.
    Yesterday we went to the "Cellar"
for coffee – very welcoming. After
lunch we set off in the van & had
a look at Ellesmere College – a
Woodard school, & pleasant. We
walked back along the canal to
the Ellesmere town playground
which the children enjoyed. Eve
went of shopping on her own.

The "Cllers" could not have us for supper, so we went to an Indian Tandoori restaurant. Delicious food. We all wrapped up warm for the night – closing vents, wearing hats. Eve wore her waistcoat upsidedown, legs through armholes, & kept her aching hips warm. We all slept better.
      Church at 10.15., a walk up the road.
      Thomas practised his violin, (because he has an exam next week) with his music propped up inside the engine. At the end of the piece I said "Well done", but there was a sound of clapping behind the hedge, & 2 women complimented him on his playing.
      Church was very friendly, a sermon on the challenge of being disciples of Jesus – a cross in the box allusion to the coming general election.
